description:
Make sync commit messages on refs/heads/beads/store more descriptive. Instead of 'sync: +1 created', show what actually changed:

- created bd-xxx: "Title here"
- updated bd-yyy desc: "new description"  
- closed bd-zzz: "reason"
- deleted bd-abc

This helps with debugging and auditing bead changes over time. Since these commits live on a separate ref (beads/store), they won't clutter the main code history.
title: Smart commit messages from beads context

description: Provide in-memory WalIndex (no sqlite) and test-harness wiring for deterministic, no-fsync test stores.
title: In-memory WAL/index backend for tests

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] Git store meta parsing gates on `format_version` via a supported-meta type.
- [ ] Checkpoint import rejects unsupported `checkpoint_format_version` for both file and export paths.
- [ ] Downstream code accepts only supported-meta types (no raw version fields).
- [ ] Tests cover unsupported version rejection and supported version acceptance.
description:
**Problem**
Format/version gating is optional across sync/checkpoint layers:
- Git store `parse_meta` returns `format_version` but callers do not enforce it (e.g., `crates/beads-rs/src/git/sync.rs`).
- Checkpoint import does not check `checkpoint_format_version` at all in `crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/import.rs`.

This is a global invariant that is enforced ad-hoc, creating drift risk and silent incompatibilities.

**Files:**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/git/sync.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/import.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/meta.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/publish.rs`
design:
**Design**
Introduce typed "supported meta" gates so anything past parsing is guaranteed to be on a supported version.

Concrete plan:
1) Add `SupportedStoreMeta` and `SupportedCheckpointMeta` types with constructors that validate format versions against constants.
2) Change `parse_meta` to either return `SupportedStoreMeta` or provide `parse_supported_meta` used everywhere.
3) Add version gating to checkpoint import (both disk-based and in-memory export) using `SupportedCheckpointMeta`.
4) Make all downstream code accept only the supported meta types so version checks are impossible to skip.

**Design Notes**
- Keep error messages explicit about expected vs actual versions.
- When new versions are introduced, add the gate once in the supported meta constructors.
title: Gate store/checkpoint meta on supported versions

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The shared git template fixture no longer leaks kept tempdirs
- [ ] Template reuse still avoids repeated cold repo setup inside a test binary
- [ ] Repeated nextest runs do not accumulate stray template repos
description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/git.rs` keeps the cached template repo path instead of owning the tempdir for the process lifetime. Repeated nextest runs leak one real repo per test binary invocation under the system temp root.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/git.rs`
design: Cache an owned `TempDir` (or equivalent fixture-owned temp root) for the lifetime of the test process instead of calling `keep()`. Preserve the current template-reuse behavior without leaving unmanaged repos behind.
title: Stop leaking kept template repos from the integration git fixture
